cultureIntro: Vencore is the design-forward arm of a construction house with roots since the 1970s. We hire people who care about craft, accountability, and the discipline of delivery — not just beautiful drawings.

pillars.title: Design with buildability
pillars.text: Every concept is tested against site reality, vendor capability, and programme constraints before it reaches the client.

pillars.title: One accountable desk
pillars.text: Design, procurement, and site leadership sit under one leadership team — no hand-offs between siloed agencies.

pillars.title: Programmes that ship
pillars.text: From villas to corporate floors, our teams see projects through to snag-free handover — not just concept presentations.

values.title: Ownership
values.text: You are trusted with real scope, real sites, and real client conversations — early in your tenure.

values.title: Precision
values.text: Drawings, BOQs, and site checks are held to a standard that protects programme and margin.

values.title: Calm under pressure
values.text: Launch dates slip when teams panic. We reward clarity, preparation, and steady site leadership.

values.title: Growth through exposure
values.text: Residential, corporate, hospitality — you will work across typologies with senior mentorship.

roles.title: Interior Designer (Mid)
roles.summary: Lead design development for premium residential and commercial programmes — from spatial planning through material boards, 3D visualization, and site coordination.
responsibilities: Develop concept, schematic, and detailed interior layouts aligned with client brief and buildability
Prepare material palettes, joinery details, and lighting intent for client presentations
Coordinate with execution teams on site dimensions, MEP interfaces, and mock-up approvals
Support BOQ preparation and vendor sampling with procurement
requirements: 3–6 years in interior design or architecture (residential / commercial preferred)
Proficiency in AutoCAD, SketchUp or equivalent, and presentation tools
Strong material knowledge — stone, veneers, metal, textiles, and lighting
Ability to communicate design intent clearly to clients and site teams
niceToHave: Enscape, V-Ray, or similar visualization workflow

roles.title: Project Engineer
roles.summary: Own day-to-day site execution for turnkey fit-out programmes — schedule control, vendor coordination, quality checks, and snag close-out.
responsibilities: Lead on-ground execution across civil, MEP, joinery, and finishing trades
Maintain daily site logs, progress reports, and deviation tracking
Coordinate vendor mobilization, material deliveries, and mock-up sign-offs
Support PMO with snag lists, punch items, and handover documentation
requirements: 3–5 years in interior fit-out, construction, or MEP coordination
Ability to read architectural and interior working drawings
Experience managing multiple subcontractors on active sites
Diploma or degree in civil, mechanical, or related engineering discipline
niceToHave: Experience in occupied commercial towers or premium residences
Basic understanding of HVAC, electrical, and plumbing coordination
Proficiency in MS Project or similar scheduling tools

roles.title: Business Development Executive
roles.summary: Build and nurture client relationships across corporate, residential, and developer segments — from first briefing through proposal support and programme onboarding.
responsibilities: Identify and qualify opportunities across Hyderabad's design-build market
Lead client briefings, site visits, and proposal coordination with design leads
Maintain pipeline visibility and CRM hygiene for leadership review
Support RFP responses, capability presentations, and reference programme tours
requirements: 2–5 years in B2B sales, real estate, or design-build business development
Strong written and verbal communication — comfortable with senior client stakeholders
Understanding of interior fit-out scope, timelines, and commercial structures
niceToHave: Existing network in corporate real estate or premium residential
Experience with turnkey vs design-only commercial models
Background in architecture, interiors, or construction

process.title: Application
process.text: Submit your CV, portfolio link, and a short note on why Vencore — via our enquiry form or email.

process.title: Review
process.text: Our leadership desk reviews every application personally. Shortlisted candidates hear back within 5 business days.

process.title: Conversation
process.text: A 45–60 minute discussion on your work, approach, and the programmes you would join.

process.title: Offer
process.text: Successful candidates receive a structured offer with role clarity, reporting line, and onboarding plan.

benefits: Direct mentorship from founders and practice leads

faqs.a: We occasionally take on exceptional junior designers and site engineers as trainees. Most open roles expect 2+ years of relevant experience, but we welcome strong portfolios and a clear point of view.
Design roles include studio time and mandatory site visits. Execution roles are primarily on-site. We do not offer fully remote positions at this time.

faqs.q: What should I include in my application?
faqs.a: CV, portfolio or project sheet (PDF or link), current compensation expectations, and notice period. For design roles, include process work — not just renders.

faqs.q: How long does the hiring process take?
faqs.a: Typically 2–3 weeks from application to offer for active roles. We move faster when programme timelines require immediate onboarding.
